title: baccharis incarum var. lejia
intro: Baccharis incarum var. lejia, also known as Peruvian lavender, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae, native to the Andean region of South America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been used to treat wounds, infections, and skin conditions. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity, although further research is needed to confirm these effects. Baccharis incarum var. lejia is typically prepared as an infusion or decoction for medicinal use, and it may also be used in traditional herbal remedies and folk medicine.
